title: Meet the woman tasked with making our province work
bodyText:
PROVINCIAL NEWS - Western Cape Provincial Minister of Infrastructure, Tertuis Simmers has announced the appointment of Advocate Chantal Smith as the new Head of the Western Cape Department of Infrastructure.

He says Smith brings an impressive track record of legal expertise and a commitment to public service.


> "Her experience and expertise will be invaluable as we continue to develop and enhance the province's infrastructure development drive."


The former CFO of the Department of Mobility will officially occupy her new position from 1 August.

“With Advocate Smith's extensive experience in the public sector, coupled with her deep understanding of our province's unique challenges and opportunities, she is an excellent choice for this critical role. Her leadership and vision will be instrumental in driving forward our ambitious infrastructure projects, ensuring that they are delivered efficiently and effectively, and that they meet the highest standards of quality and sustainability,” Simmers says.

“I have full confidence in Advocate Smith's ability to lead the Department of Infrastructure with integrity, dedication, and a clear focus on the needs and aspirations of our communities. Together, we will work to build a more connected, resilient, and prosperous province for all our residents. We look forward to her contributions and to the positive impact she will undoubtedly have.”

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
